title: The Opening
text:
The Priest begins:

In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit for ever.

The Congregation responds:

Amen.

title: Glory be to the Father
text:
Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit;
From everlasting to everlasting. Amen and Amen.

title: The Lord's Prayer (with Qanona)
text:
The Lord's Prayer is said, farced with the Qanona (the refrain):

Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name. Your kingdom come.
Holy, holy, holy are you, our Father in heaven. Heaven and earth are full of the greatness of your glory. Angels and men cry out to you: Holy, holy, holy are you.
Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name. Your kingdom come. Your will be done on earth as in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread. And forgive us our trespasses, as we forgive those who trespass against us. And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one. For yours is the kingdom, and the power, and the glory, for ever and ever. Amen.

title: Laku Mara (To You, Lord)
text:
The Laku Mara, the Hymn of the Resurrection, is chanted:

To you, Lord of all, we give thanks;
And you, Jesus Christ, we glorify;
For you are the restorer of our bodies,
And you are the savior of our souls.

title: The Trisagion
text:
The Trisagion is chanted three times:

Holy God,
Holy Mighty,
Holy Immortal,
Have mercy on us.

title: The Marmitha
text: [The appointed Marmitha of the Psalter is recited here.]

title: The Suraya
text:
The Suraya is chanted:

Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path.
I have sworn an oath and confirmed it, to observe your righteous ordinances.
I am severely afflicted; give me life, O Lord, according to your word.

title: The Karozutha (Litany)
text:
The Deacon intones the Karozutha. The Congregation responds: Lord, have mercy.

Let us all stand well in sorrow and care and say: Lord, have mercy.
Father of mercies and God of all comfort, we beseech you: Lord, have mercy.
For the peace, harmony, and well-being of the whole world and of all churches, we beseech you: Lord, have mercy.
For our country and for all countries, and for those who dwell in them in faith, we beseech you: Lord, have mercy.

title: The Concluding Blessing
text:
The Priest blesses the congregation:

May God, the Lord of all, bless us. May He keep our days in peace, and establish our lives in His grace and mercy, now and always, and for ever and ever.

The Congregation responds:

Amen.

title: The Suraya
text:
The Suraya is chanted:

Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path.
I have sworn an oath and confirmed it, to observe your righteous ordinances.
I am severely afflicted; give me life, O Lord, according to your word.

title: The Karozutha (Litany)
text:
The Deacon intones the Karozutha. The Congregation responds: Lord, have mercy.

Let us all stand well in sorrow and care and say: Lord, have mercy.
Father of mercies and God of all comfort, we beseech you: Lord, have mercy.
For the peace, harmony, and well-being of the whole world and of all churches, we beseech you: Lord, have mercy.
For our country and for all countries, and for those who dwell in them in faith, we beseech you: Lord, have mercy.

title: The Concluding Blessing
text:
The Priest blesses the congregation:

May God, the Lord of all, bless us. May He keep our days in peace, and establish our lives in His grace and mercy, now and always, and for ever and ever.

The Congregation responds:

Amen.

title: The Morning Hymn
text:
The Morning Hymn is chanted:

Light has dawned for the righteous, and joy for the upright in heart.
Christ our hope has risen from the dead; He has delivered us from darkness and given us light.

title: The Karozutha (Morning Litany)
text:
The Deacon intones the Morning Karozutha:

Let us all stand well in joy and gladness and say: Lord, have mercy.
For the peace of the whole world and the preservation of the holy churches, we beseech you: Lord, have mercy.
O Christ, who are the true light, shine in our hearts that we may walk in your truth: Lord, have mercy.

title: The 'Onitha (Anthem)
text:
The appointed seasonal anthem is sung:

Blessed is Christ, who has magnified His holy Church, and established her upon the rock of faith. By His grace, she sings praise to His name.

title: The 'Onitha (Anthem for the Great Fast)
text:
The appointed seasonal anthem is sung:

O Lord, open the door of your mercy to our prayers, and accept our fasting and our tears. Forgive our debts, O good and merciful God.

title: The 'Onitha (Anthem for the Resurrection)
text:
The appointed seasonal anthem is sung:

Christ is risen from the dead! He has overthrown the power of death and raised all of creation with Him. Let the heavens and the earth rejoice!

title: The Night Prayer
text:
The Priest prays:

Strengthen us, O our Lord and God, that we may be vigilant before You in pure prayer during the night watches. May we sing Your praises with the heavenly hosts, now and always, and for ever and ever.

title: The Tishbohta (Hymn of Praise)
text:
The Tishbohta is sung:

Blessed is the glorious honor of the Lord from His place.
From everlasting to everlasting, You alone are God.
Let all creation praise Your holy name.
